statements.1:
Goal

You are given N Sudoku puzzles, each with a 9×9 grid. Output a single answer board whose digits fill the empty cells of every puzzle, getting each row, column and 3×3 block as close to a complete 1…9 as possible.
How your board is applied
For each puzzle, every empty cell takes the digit on your board at the same position; givens stay untouched. If you wrote . at a position where at least one puzzle is still empty, that puzzle keeps a hole and your whole submission fails.
Scoring
For each row, column and 3×3 block of every puzzle, count m = the number of digits 1…9 missing from that row / column / block and add m2 to the total.
For example, 123123123 is missing 6 digits and contributes 36 (6²); 548163972 contributes 0.
Lower is better; a perfect set of solutions scores 0.
Input
Line 1: the integer N, the number of puzzles.

Next 9×N lines: the N puzzles, one after another. Each puzzle is 9 lines of 9 characters: . for an empty cell, 1…9 for a given digit.
Output
9 lines of 9 characters — your answer board.
Write the digit 1…9 you want placed at that position, or . if every puzzle already has that cell filled.
The answer board itself does not have to be a valid Sudoku — the same digit may appear several times in a row, column or 3×3 block.
Constraints
1 ≤ N ≤ 20
Every given puzzle is a valid (partial) Sudoku — no row, column or 3×3 block contains the same digit twice.
Allotted response time to output is ≤ 1s.
